title: German Dub of Q
wikitext:
[]: German Dub of Q             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Dec 13, 2013 1:21 pm My pre-ordered BluRay* arrived today. Yay! Unfortunately, no interviews in the booklet and a rather obvious gaffe on the cover summary, but It never was any better. Strangely, there were explanations on the making of/animation process, tho. Still. It kinda annoys me that the trailer was put untranslated into the extra section instead of at the end of the film - some people may not actually already know what the trailer says, and it just breaks symmetry with the series and the other two movies, where they DID include the trailer. As for the dub itself, it was...acceptable. Not really outstanding or anywhere near the brilliantness of the original, but not bad, either (except for one detail I will elaborate on later) It certainly was respectful, many important lines where simply left as they werein the original or would be rendered in a fansub, like Asuka and Mari's talk about 'saving', Mari's final speech and Fuyutsuki's possibly telling Gendo related line, so, plus points for that. There's nothing to inspire wonder about the dub itself, but for the most part, it serves its purpose in conveying the original with an acceptable degree of faithfulness and respect. Generally, it was just great to see it on a proper huge HD screen and notice/enjoy details like the AT-Field barrier shattering in the panorama shot of the Wunder emerging, the lovingly rendered night sky/interplay of sky shots, that shot of Shinji seen between the piano and its lid, the brightness of operation US, EVA 13's reflection on the seal, the Wunder bridge revealing Misato at the very top of the tower, the core effect in the credits... Voice Acting Let's start straight to the one thing I really disliked: Asuka's new VA dindn't convince me in 2.0, and she isn't any better in 3.0. As far as I can discern, she went for a bit of a 'uncaring coldly indifferent moody teenager' approach, but there wasn't that much of a concious attempt to differentiate pre- and post timeskip Asuka, this might as well be pre-timeskip, or classic series Asuka on a bad day. It's not that she didn't try to act, but she never lost that artifical, tacked-on quality and often lacked intensity, most glaringly in the "Do something, baka-Shinji!" incident. Depending on your interpretation, she's either revealing a crucial bit of hidden feelings or (most likely) cursing at the one who threw her in that horrible situation as her EVA breaks appart around her. In any case, it should be intense. There should be a climax in the arch of her cadence. Kaworu's VA, on the other hand, just nailed it from beginning to end. Somewhat less solemly ceremonial/carriedly high-brow-y than his tv predecessor, but a just as valid aura of overarthly untouchedness. This boy is randomly there, his colors don't fit with the gloomy world around him, he walks unprotected through the poisonous atmosphere like a phantasm, and he shares wisdom in a light, gently leading voice. Yes. Mission acomplished. Other pleasant surprises where the actors of Hideki, Midori and Kouji, you're almost sad they didn't get more scenes; They gave them as much personality (like the youth and inexperiencedness of the former two) as those few technobabble allow. Sumire's wasn't quite up to the standart - first it's a very different kind of voice, not deep or smoky at all, and at times just too much "generic chick" instead of technical and cool. How they rendered her line about using the main engine didn't help either, they turned it into, "I'M not ready for this, I'm not confident enough." instead of "I'm not confident in [untested system]" But there were also things that stood out positively, like, despite her relatively small role, Ritsuko. Maybe she used up all the "cool technicality" they had. Then again, she is one of the returning actresses. I think. Can't remember right now. XD Oh, if only they could've kept the entire cast from the series. At least they kept Hannes Maurer for the most demanding part. He wasn't afraid to properly sob and do the shaky voice when it was needed, without overdoing it. It was mostly an attempt to convey/copy the original, I must particularly applaud the reaction to fourth impact breaking loose. Mari's Va was also mostly copying the original, one or two moments Did go into overly silly or not taken seriously territory, but thankfully, the crucial moments were faithfully rendered, including the fourth impact plunge. Sakura was nice, too. The various aspects where there, maybe a little less of the 'clam' part, but there are lines where omitting it is impossible, so it was there. The little sound she makes when she makes that little fist :( Misato was... hm. There are these interview about how Anno told Mitsuishi to just "talk naturally" (ie, her actual age), which she couldn't do (probably because Mitsuishi is a nice person with a big heart for unfortunate 14 year old draftees) but I feel the german dub was a bit like what the possible result of that order could have been like. It's not that there's no differentiation, there's some in the voice itself, but the way it gets used isn't that unlike regular regular business mode Misato. That doesn't mean that ice wasn't present when the line couldn't do without it, like the "from now on, you won't do anything at all" (and yes, that's how it was rendered. "Von nun an... wirst du gar nichts mehr tun!") There where a few scenes involving Shinji where I wasn't sure wether it's supposed to be lingering affection or just her saying thing meaningfully. Probably the latter, because if its the former, the placement would be quiet random. They did give weight, even a few lines that she says in a quieter, more feminine voice to the scene where Ritsuko states that all they can do against the nemesis series is retreat, and there she admits her situation, but, in this voice that's as thin and bony as her fingers, with more of a cold sobriety than the hammy, glorious declaration in other versions, says that she will turn the situation around. The significance WILLE's strugle, and the grey nature of the situation is being aknowledged. Now for ReiQ. Hm. Unclear. Mixed bag. At times it felt like the actress just kept playing regular Rei, just in a scene where she is supposed to be neutral or indifferent. . The "alienness" of regular Rei is often overstated, there's a lot more of cadence and lowscale reaction than she gets credit for, so that performance was good when it was regular Rei/Rei II, but for ReiQ it doesn't fit, there should be more apathy. This also has the side-effect of her not really sounding that noticeably off when she says her first few "come here"s. Maybe to not spoil the surprise? It was only in the conversation in her room that she gave ReiQ a bit of her own differentiating quality, mostly a bit more high and open. There were a few scenes where the tone of voice could be misunderstood, like this exchange: "So Ayanami Rei would read books?" "Eh, yes, you had them etc." "Yes?" The translator obviously intended that to be a questioning/unknowing "Yes?", not a "Yes...?" There isn't supposed to be recognition. Maybe the actress thought she was supposed to do it abit ep 23 like. Dunno. Or "This was my order." The 'this' is emphasized in a way that it looks like she's still argeuing with Mari. "No, dummy, I'm not supposed to flee, I'm supposed to decapitate this EVA. Silly you for suggesting anything else." Maybe I just can't into Japanese tone, but to me, the original ReiQ sounded more like she was saying why she was decapitating the EVA (orders) not any longer comversing on what she was or wasn't ordered to do. But this is just as bit of criticism, it wasn't, like, extreme/glaring. Translation & terminology The DSS Choker was rendered as having been built for Kaworu. It does say " deleted memories" and the word clone is outright used. "Adam's vessel" sounds rather cool in German. ("Adamsgefäß") What Mari says to ReiQ is translated as roughly "Don't you wanna leave the Adam's vessel before you merge with it?" (implying a risk of involuntary absorption) Also, her comment at seeing the 12th angel is: "Also, I've always wondered if the devil himself will show up after all angels are killed..." Possible mixup between "aku" (evil) and "akuma" (demon/devil) or metaphoric liberty? What does she actually say in Japanese? It sounds kind of cool, tho. Also, Asuka does say "Shut up and die!" during her battle with Shinji. What she says there, "taosu" is something like "defeat" is it? Hence, "let yourself be defeated" ~~ "go down quietly/yield", but I've seen dat used in many shonen anime where the reciepient is very surely not indended to survive the defeating. In any case, she was charging him with a spear. Still, I'd be careful with taking away degrees of interpretation room before the fat lady sings in the fourth film. Speaking of which, they managed to match up "in exchange for new life..." with the merging FoIs. As for those, German!Kaworu simply explains the things on the walls of the main shaft as, "Yes. Those are the remains of those who couldn't complete their complementation/instrumentality." Also interesting is what they did with what always seemed to be this huge dodgy line about "reparation for your sin", something like "they have marked you for [sin etc]" ("gezeichnet") evoking the whole concept of a literal "stigma" Sakura's final line was something like "Spare us of that!" or "Don't do that to us" ("Erspar uns das!") Since "2-dash" would sound kinda clunky, they went with "Unit 2.1" indeed, punning on the titles a bit which is... actually quite cool. Cool enough to use in fanfic. Also, nifty touch to have Gendo quote genesis as he turns off Seele. "We saw what was done, and it was good." Very modest guy, this Gendo fellow. Reminds me of when they had him quote "homo homini lupus" in episode 11, it simply suits the concept he's adressing. In both cases. But there were also less sucessful attempts. Shinji: "Why shouldn't I leave? You don't need me!" Dub!Misato: "But you need US. To protect you." "protective custody", or what politicians commonly call that way, is quite a different thing from "We'll protect you!" In any case, original!Misato is not saying "mamoru" or similar verbs. Or maybe it was meant to sound hypocritical to the audience? A lesser issue is where Asuka tells Mari to "take care of the [brat]" instead of "the brat's eva", which would be a minor mouth flap matching thing if the word choice didn't have this potentiasl to be possibly (though not necessarily) misunderstood as "kill him." I mean, I think we are meant to understand Mari's actions as her sparing him, but it would shine a distinctly different light on everyone involved if she was defying orders here. Neither does Asuka say something like, "What, you're alive?" But maybe I'm just overracted, sometimes "take care of it" just means "take care of it." The nicknames were also a tad graceless (but they have been since 2.0) "prinzesschen" was also how they rendered the way Asuka herself called Rei pre elevator scene, so this may look like a parallel where none should be - it was just bad luck, I guess. But the other two are complete trainwrecks. At least Mari's. They went with "Glasses-rella" which, admittely sounds less clunky when "Glasses" has a double L in it, but... come on. It's silly. I know you meant to be witty by lampshading Mari's "manic pixie girl" attributes, but a simple "Vierauge" or "Brillenschlange" (existing RL variations of "four-eyes") would have done the trick as well. Asuka's supposed to be an adult professional fighter now, damnit. Also, the rendering of "gaki" as "quälgeist" (literally, "tormenting little spirit", but mostly used figuratively for "nuisance","annoyance" or "pest" in the sense you'd apply these words to a person.) is... I won't say it's too harsh, the woman habitually kicks him like a football, but it's... unspecific. Yes, that word is often used for annoying children, but it doesn't necessarily denote that, it can be "annoying person" in a much broader context. A degree of metaphor/subtext is lost, the implication of an accusation of naivité and the newly established age gap (which the actress generally ignored, see above) should be there. I would have suggested the expression "Dummes Kind", which is appropiately despective and commes with these connotations of "incapable failure", or at least play it safe and simply use "Bengel" (literally "brat"), it even has the same amount of syllabes. and, because I'm a nitpicking meanie, don't say "flywheel". More nautic terms, less "english is so much cooler/ no one wants to hear your ugly nazi modor speak anyway." You're *representing* our language comunity here. I'm very glad you're not the respectless meddling murricans, but ph-lease, you can overdo it both ways. ________________________________ *(I would say bluRei, but the movie doesn't have any actual Rei in it. BluTentativeRei? QuRei?)

[]: Re: German Dub of Q             Quote Post by qu4d » Fri Dec 13, 2013 1:30 pm Kendrix wrote: Voice Acting Let's start straight to the one thing I really disliked: Asuka's new VA dindn't convince me in 2.0, and she isn't any better in 3.0.

[]: Re: German Dub of Q             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Dec 13, 2013 1:37 pm qu4d wrote: New VA? Isn't it Julia Ziffer in both NGE and NME?!

[]: Re: German Dub of Q             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Dec 13, 2013 2:01 pm Kendrix wrote: her comment at seeing the 12th angel is: "Also, I've always wondered if the devil himself will show up after all angels are killed..." Possible mixup between "aku" (evil) and "akuma" (demon/devil) or metaphoric liberty? What does she actually say in Japanese? It sounds kind of cool, tho.

[]: Re: German Dub of Q             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 13, 2013 2:14 pm Kendrix wrote: "Adam's vessel" sounds rather cool in German.

[]: Re: German Dub of Q             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Dec 13, 2013 3:02 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Yarareru is what's used in the original Japanese.

[]: Re: German Dub of Q             Quote Post by Merkaba » Fri Dec 13, 2013 10:07 pm Kendrix wrote: I'm very glad you're not the respectless meddling murricans

[]: Re: German Dub of Q             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 13, 2013 11:01 pm Merkaba wrote: This is actually a very cool line, whether or not it's completely faithful to the original. I hope the AMERICAN-produced dub has something similar.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Dec 14, 2013 1:39 am Considering that there are literally things called angels in the americanized version (they're still called apostles or messengers or something in Japanese, aren't they?) keeping the devil reference in makes perfect sense.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Dec 14, 2013 3:18 am Puns are all well and good, but I'd rather the idiom was translated to idiom.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Sat Dec 14, 2013 4:32 am We are talking about a dub that keeps honorifics and pronounces them as Shinji-KIIIN

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 14, 2013 7:07 am The pronunciation was only that grating for 1.11, they got mostly tolerable after that. They're overcompensating with the names, tho. The original dub didn't know which vocals to drop/underplay, this one overdoes it, and the actual pronunciation would probably not be that different from how the vocals and and consonants are in German, just without the "long words have the emphasis on the second syllabe" thing. But I guess you can't expect them to do it perfectly any more than you can expect Miyamura's German to be intelligible. As for the suffixes, today, most of the ppl who watch anime will be familiar with these anyway, evangelion is not exactly what some 12 year old would watch as their first anime, and a faction of the fans gets pedantic over those. That said, the dub for the original series did drop them and have the kids refer to each other with their first names, but Rebuild simply happened to make the way people call each other a plotpoint, so you're screwed either way... I'm personally fairly apathetic about it/don't mind either way. As for Mari's line, I think the "devil" is, in context, sufficiently understandable as metaphorical, ie "any sort of unforseen bad things could happen" There's another potentially problematic line, tho, when Shinji encounters ReiQ. So far, all translations I've seen have that as "How long has it been since you left unit one?" *marked lack of answer*, not "...Well, I guess it's only natural that you don't know, you were also only just retrieved from unit one after all." *no comment* Besides being incorrect, Shinji's exact degree of cluelessness and conciousness inside unit one is potentially relevant to assesing his culpability. If he can't even tell when/if Rei left, he clearly had no perception of "spending time in there with her in happyland and not caring to come back". Also, it would make little sense for Shinji to assume they were retrieved at the same time, since, if they were retrieved at the same time, how would Rei have gotten to NeoNerv so fast when Shinji only just regained conciousness? Yeah. --- Oh, and before I forget it, another potentially interesting translation: Kaworu's comment on Shinji's Stargargazing hobby. "I see, instead of a world that's always changing, you prefer the uncaring, all-eveloping emptiness..." which sounds somewhat less assholish than other translation. But it might as well be them who got it wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Sat Dec 14, 2013 7:45 am Kendrix wrote: As for the suffixes, today, most of the ppl who watch anime will be familiar with these anyway, evangelion is not exactly what some 12 year old would watch as their first anime, and a faction of the fans gets pedantic over those.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 14, 2013 7:56 am A.T. Fish wrote: If someone is that much of a weeaboo they'd be watching it subbed in the first place

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Sat Dec 14, 2013 8:07 am Reichu wrote: Do people even know what "weeaboo" (= "wapanese") means anymore?

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Sat Dec 14, 2013 11:13 am Chuckman wrote: Considering that there are literally things called angels in the americanized version (they're still called apostles or messengers or something in Japanese, aren't they?) keeping the devil reference in makes perfect sense.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Dec 14, 2013 11:21 am ^ In Japanese they're called "Shito", which could mean "messengers" or "apostles"), but Gainax gave many official English terms via side material(books, interviews...) and their official English name is indeed "Angel".

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Sat Dec 14, 2013 11:29 am Rei IV wrote: What? This is the first time I've heard of this because merchandise and the like officially call the alien-like beings hell bent on reaching Lilith "Angels". It certainly wasn't made up by Americans or anything like that unless I'm missing something.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Dec 14, 2013 12:19 pm pwhodges wrote: Puns are all well and good, but I'd rather the idiom was translated to idiom.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 14, 2013 1:04 pm Chuckman wrote: Because a random comment about ogres and snakes sounds like gibberish and talking about the devil sounds badass. Haven't you ever seen The Usual Suspects ?

Unfortunately, no interviews in the booklet and a rather obvious gaffe on the cover summary, but It never was any better. Strangely, there were explanations on the making of/animation process, tho. Shinji: "Why shouldn't I leave? You don't need me!" Dub!Misato: "But you need US. To protect you." "protective custody", or what politicians commonly call that way, is quite a different thing from "We'll protect you!" In any case, original!Misato is not saying "mamoru" or similar verbs. Or maybe it was meant to sound hypocritical to the audience? A lesser issue is where Asuka tells Mari to "take care of the [brat]" instead of "the brat's eva", which would be a minor mouth flap matching thing if the word choice didn't have this potentiasl to be possibly (though not necessarily) misunderstood as "kill him." I mean, I think we are meant to understand Mari's actions as her sparing him, but it would shine a distinctly different light on everyone involved if she was defying orders here. Neither does Asuka say something like, "What, you're alive?" But maybe I'm just overracted, sometimes "take care of it" just means "take care of it." The nicknames were also a tad graceless (but they have been since 2.0) "prinzesschen" was also how they rendered the way Asuka herself called Rei pre elevator scene, so this may look like a parallel where none should be - it was just bad luck, I guess. But the other two are complete trainwrecks. At least Mari's. They went with "Glasses-rella" which, admittely sounds less clunky when "Glasses" has a double L in it, but... come on. It's silly. I know you meant to be witty by lampshading Mari's "manic pixie girl" attributes, but a simple "Vierauge" or "Brillenschlange" (existing RL variations of "four-eyes") would have done the trick as well. Asuka's supposed to be an adult professional fighter now, damnit. Also, the rendering of "gaki" as "quälgeist" (literally, "tormenting little spirit", but mostly used figuratively for "nuisance","annoyance" or "pest" in the sense you'd

Article: German Dub of Q
 Also, the rendering of "gaki" as "quälgeist" (literally, "tormenting little spirit", but mostly used figuratively for "nuisance","annoyance" or "pest" in the sense you'd apply these words to a person.) is... I won't say it's too harsh, the woman habitually kicks him like a football, but it's... unspecific. Yes, that word is often used for annoying children, but it doesn't necessarily denote that, it can be "annoying person" in a much broader context. A degree of metaphor/subtext is lost, the implication of an accusation of naivité and the newly established age gap (which the actress generally ignored, see above) should be there. I would have suggested the expression "Dummes Kind", which is appropiately despective and commes with these connotations of "incapable failure", or at least play it safe and simply use "Bengel" (literally "brat"), it even has the same amount of syllabes. and, because I'm a nitpicking meanie, don't say "flywheel". More nautic terms, less "english is so much cooler/ no one wants to hear your ugly nazi modor speak anyway." You're *representing* our language comunity here. I'm very glad you're not the respectless meddling murricans, but ph-lease, you can overdo it both ways. ________________________________ *(I would say bluRei, but the movie doesn't have any actual Rei in it. BluTentativeRei? QuRei?)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 14, 2013 7:07 am The pronunciation was only that grating for 1.11, they got mostly tolerable after that. They're overcompensating with the names, tho. The original dub didn't know which vocals to drop/underplay, this one overdoes it, and the actual pronunciation would probably not be that different from how the vocals and and consonants are in German, just without the "long words have the emphasis on the second syllabe" thing. But I guess you can't expect them to do it perfectly any more than you can expect Miyamura's German to be intelligible. As for the suffixes, today, most of the ppl who watch anime will be familiar with these anyway, evangelion is not exactly what some 12 year old would watch as their first anime, and a faction of the fans gets pedantic over those. That said, the dub for the original series did drop them and have the kids refer to each other with their first names, but Rebuild simply happened to make the way people call each other a plotpoint, so you're screwed either way... I'm personally fairly apathetic about it/don't mind either way. As for Mari's line, I think the "devil" is, in context, sufficiently understandable as metaphorical, ie "any sort of unforseen bad things could happen" There's another potentially problematic line, tho, when Shinji encounters ReiQ. So far, all translations I've seen have that as "How long has it been since you left unit one?" *marked lack of answer*, not "...Well, I guess it's only natural that you don't know, you were also only just retrieved from unit one after all." *no comment* Besides being incorrect, Shinji's exact degree of cluelessness and conciousness inside unit one is potentially relevant to assesing his culpability. If he can't even tell when/if Rei left, he clearly had no perception of "spending time in there with her in happyland and not caring to come back". Also, it would make little sense for Shinji to assume they were retrieved at the same time, since, if they were retrieved at the same time, how would Rei have

 time in there with her in happyland and not caring to come back".
